reasoning: Hallucinations in LLMs are widely recognized by experts as a fundamental feature of probabilistic autoregressive text generation rather than a solvable bug. Scientific papers from 2025 mathematically argue that hallucinations are inevitable due to learning limits, and while mitigation techniques like RAG, guardrails, and CoT have reduced their frequency, they have not and cannot completely eliminate them. Given that the knowledge cutoff is merely two months before resolution and hallucinations are still an active area of mitigation research rather than a solved problem, it is virtually impossible that the issue will be universally declared "fixed" by the end of 2025.
